original_query: select s_name, s_address from supplier, nation where s_suppkey in ( select ps_suppkey from partsupp where ps_partkey in ( select p_partkey from part where p_name like 'burnished%' ) and ps_availqty > ( select 0.5 * sum(l_quantity) from lineitem where l_partkey = ps_partkey and l_suppkey = ps_suppkey and l_shipdate >= date '1996-01-01' and l_shipdate < date '1996-01-01' + interval '1' year ) ) and s_nationkey = n_nationkey and n_name = 'GERMANY' order by s_name;
rewritten_query:
WITH lineitem_sum AS (
    SELECT l_partkey, l_suppkey, SUM(l_quantity) AS total_qty
    FROM lineitem
    WHERE l_shipdate >= DATE '1996-01-01' AND l_shipdate < DATE '1997-01-01'
    GROUP BY l_partkey, l_suppkey
),
burnished_parts AS (
    SELECT p_partkey
    FROM part
    WHERE p_name LIKE 'burnished%'
)
SELECT s.s_name, s.s_address
FROM supplier s
JOIN nation n ON s.s_nationkey = n.n_nationkey
WHERE n.n_name = 'GERMANY'
  AND EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM partsupp ps
    JOIN burnished_parts bp ON ps.ps_partkey = bp.p_partkey
    JOIN lineitem_sum ls ON ps.ps_partkey = ls.l_partkey AND ps.ps_suppkey = ls.l_suppkey
    WHERE ps.ps_suppkey = s.s_suppkey
      AND ps.ps_availqty > 0.5 * ls.total_qty
  )
ORDER BY s.s_name;

rewrite_suggestion.produced_suggestion: Replaced correlated subquery with a pre-computed CTE (lineitem_sum) to avoid repeated calculations
Created a CTE for burnished parts to filter early and reduce the number of rows processed
Converted IN clauses to EXISTS with JOINs to improve performance while maintaining the same logic
Used explicit JOIN syntax instead of implicit joins in the FROM clause for better readability and maintainability
